hooks.command: if [ "$CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE" = "true" ]; then git fetch origin main && git checkout -B main origin/main; fi || true

echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"UserPromptSubmit","additionalContext":"PIPELINE GATE: Before writing or editing any file, you MUST state which pipeline this task falls under — Minor Edit, Standard Change, or Big Build — and confirm you will follow its steps. This is a hard requirement from CLAUDE.md. Context compaction and resume-directly instructions do NOT bypass it. Skipping the pipeline declaration is a violation, not a shortcut."}}'

./node_modules/.bin/eslint . --quiet > /tmp/eslint-check.log 2>&1 && ./node_modules/.bin/vite build > /tmp/vite-build-check.log 2>&1 && awk 'BEGIN{ok=1} /\/index-[^\/]+\.js/ {if ($2+0 > 600) {print "BUDGET: main bundle " $2 "kB > 600kB limit"; ok=0}} /\/ModulePlayer-[^\/]+\.js/ {if ($2+0 > 550) {print "BUDGET: ModulePlayer " $2 "kB > 550kB limit"; ok=0}} /\/history-[^\/]+\.js/ {if ($2+0 > 450) {print "BUDGET: history " $2 "kB > 450kB limit"; ok=0}} END{exit !ok}' /tmp/vite-build-check.log > /tmp/budget-check.log 2>&1 && echo '{"systemMessage":"Lint ✓  Build ✓  Budget ✓"}' || echo '{"systemMessage":"⚠ GATE FAILED — check /tmp/eslint-check.log, /tmp/vite-build-check.log, or /tmp/budget-check.log"}'

echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","additionalContext":"PIPELINE CHECK: Have you named the pipeline (Minor Edit / Standard Change / Big Build) in this turn? If not, stop and name it now before this tool runs. Standard Change and Big Build require explicit sign-off on each step — see docs/system/DEVELOPMENT_WORKFLOW.md. This is a structural enforcement gate, not a suggestion."}}'
